ABOUT US
MAGDALENE HOUSE AUSTIN IS
A SURVIVOR-LED
THERAPEUTIC COMMUNITY
SUPPORTING WOMEN WHO
HAVE EXPERIENCED SEX
TRAFFICKING. THROUGH
LONG-TERM RESIDENTIAL
CARE, COUNSELING ACCESS,
AND LIFE AND CAREER
DEVELOPMENT, WE FOSTER
HEALING, BELONGING, AND
LASTING INDEPENDENCE.
By providing our residents with a
peer-based therapeutic
community, the survivors are
given the time, stability, and
relational support necessary to
heal. Our program is
intentionally designed for
cisgender women and relies on
shared lived experience and
identity-based peer safety as a
core element of the healing
process. This relational model
helps participants develop trust,
rebuild self-worth, and learn new
ways of relating to themselves
and others.
OUR VALUES
Magdalene House aims to
foster a community of
belonging, dignity, and
personal empowerment for
all residents.
We believe that healing
happens in the context of
connection — and that love,
compassion, and safety are
powerful forces in
reclaiming one’s sense of
self and future.
Our community focuses on holistic healing that addresses recovery f rom
trauma and addiction and provides tools to thrive in the future. As women
learn to live in a healthy community and take care of one another, they build
lifelong f riendships which will carry them throughout their lives. While
building community, they learn together how to create the lives they want
for themselves by accessing therapy, completing and advancing their
education, gaining professional development, and learning healthy
relationship skills.
VISION & MISSION
OUR VISION OUR MISSION
Magdalene House of Austin is
modeled after Thistle Farms and
Magdalene Nashville, founded by
Rev. Becca Stevens in 1997. Their
research and long-term experience
demonstrate that healing is most
effective in a supportive
community setting, where
survivors live together, build trust,
and are given the time and
stability to rebuild their lives.
We are grounded in relational
healing, where women support
one another through shared lived
experience, empathy, and
encouragement. Through our
peer-based therapeutic
community, residents are
supported in building conf idence,
self-advocacy, and professional
identity as part of their healing
journey.
HEAL . EMPOWER. THRIVE .
WHAT WE DO
TWO-YEAR RESIDENTIAL CARE
THERAPUTIC ACCESS
JOB TRAINING AND EDUCATION
GROUP SUPPORT
Survivors participate in a two-year program built on
holistic care. With basic needs such as lodging, food,
and clothing provided, residents are able to focus fully
on healing without the immediate pressures of
survival.
Residents work alongside the Therapeutic Support
Specialist to develop an individualized recovery
plan and connect with the services they need.
Additionally, they receive medical, dental, mental
health, and substance abuse counseling.
Survivors have the opportunity to pursue GED
certif ication, college courses, vocational training,
and internship programs to help them realize their
goals and build long-term economic independence
Participants grow alongside others who share
similar experiences and identity-based safety.
Through this method, they develop trust, emotional
regulation skills, daily living rhythms, and healthy
support networks.

DONATE
Paragraph about donating
VOLUNTEER
We believe that it takes community to truly heal f rom the
profound trauma of sex traff icking. If you are passionate about
helping survivors heal, we welcome you to become part of our
community! All prospective volunteers must complete the
volunteer application (click on the subheading ‘volunteer’), sign
up for phone interview, complete a background check, and
attend training.
JOIN
If you are need of assistance and resonate with our philosophy,
please click the subheading ‘join’ to complete a referral and apply
for shelter While immediate assistance is not currently available,
we will reach out once a opening is available.
HEAL . EMPOWER. THRIVE .
